recommendation.message: Your score on Variables, Types & Coercion suggests reviewing var/let/const hoisting and the falsy values list before moving on.
this binding is tricky. Re-read L10 (the 5 rules) and L11 (arrow traps) before continuing.
Async concepts need more practice. Focus on the event loop order and forEach/async pitfall.
Advanced TypeScript (generics, utility types, narrowing) is high-leverage. Practice writing Partial<T>, discriminated unions, and exhaustiveness patterns with your own code before the final quiz.

recommendation.message: TypeScript Advanced completed quickly. Consider doing the practice exercises before moving on — these patterns appear heavily in real codebases.

recommendation.message: this binding cards are being missed frequently. This is one of JavaScript's most confusing behaviors — repetition is the fix.
Closures need reinforcement. The mental model to build: a function is a value that carries its scope with it.
All modules complete. You're ready for the final assessment. The final quiz covers L01–L26 and requires 75% to pass.
